phrases.intent_en: Ask about tours of the hotel
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il y a des visites guidées du château ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess-KEEL ee ah day vee-ZEET gee-DAY doo shah-TOH
phrases.cultural_note: The Château Frontenac is the most photographed hotel in the world. Guided history tours run daily and explore its grand interiors without needing a room booking.

responses.phonetic: wee lay vee-ZEET PART duh lah-KUY
responses.local: Oui, les visites partent de l'accueil.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a table at the hotel restaurant
phrases.text_local: J'aimerais réserver une table au restaurant du château.
phrases.pronunciation: zheh-muh-REH ray-zair-VAY OON TAHBL oh res-toh-RAH doo shah-TOH
phrases.cultural_note: The 1608 Wine & Beer Bar and Champlain Restaurant are the hotel's dining options. Champlain is formal and requires advance reservations.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for the best photo spot of the château
phrases.text_local: C'est d'où qu'on prend la meilleure photo du château ?
phrases.pronunciation: say DOO kon prahn lah may-YUR fo-TOH doo shah-TOH
phrases.cultural_note: The best views are from the Terrasse Dufferin boardwalk below, or from the ferry across the St. Lawrence River from Lévis.

responses.phonetic: duh-PWEE lah tay-RASS doo-feh-RAHN zhoost ahn BAH
responses.local: Depuis la Terrasse Dufferin, juste en bas.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the terrace boardwalk conditions in winter
phrases.text_local: La Terrasse Dufferin, est-ce que c'est praticable en hiver ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah teh-RASS doo-FAIR ess-KUH say prah-tee-KAHBL ahn ee-VAIR
phrases.cultural_note: The Terrasse Dufferin is cleared in winter and features the famous toboggan slide (les glissades) — a Québec City winter tradition since the 1800s.

responses.english: The terrace is clear and walkable.
responses.phonetic: lah tay-RASS eh day-gah-ZHAY ay prah-tee-KABL
responses.local: La terrasse est dégagée et praticable.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to catch a calèche ride
phrases.text_local: Où est-ce qu'on peut prendre une calèche devant le château ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo ess-KON puh PRAHN-druh OON kah-LESH duh-VAH luh shah-TOH
phrases.cultural_note: Horse-drawn calèches depart from the Place d'Armes in front of the château and offer narrated tours of the old city walls and streets.

responses.english: In front of the château, on the terrace.
responses.phonetic: duh-VAHN luh shah-TOH soor lah tay-RASS
responses.local: Devant le château, sur la terrasse.
